(x - y){tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = x + 2y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x+2 y}{x-y}{/tex}
This is a homogenous differential equation.
Put y = vx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = v + x{tex} \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} v + x{tex} \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1+2 v}{1-v}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x{tex} \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1+2 v}{1-v}{/tex} - v
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x{tex} \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1+2 v-v+v^{2}}{1-v}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1-v}{v^{2}+v+1} d v{/tex} = {tex}\frac{d x}{x}{/tex}
Integrating on both the sides, we get 
{tex}\int \frac{1-v}{v^{2}+v+1} d v=\int \frac{d x}{x}{/tex}....(1)
Now,
{tex}\int \frac{1-v}{v^{2}+v+1} d v{/tex} = {tex}-\int \frac{v-1}{v^{2}+v+1}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 v-2}{v^{2}+v+1}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 v+1}{v^{2}+v+1} d v-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{-3 d v}{v^{2}+v+1}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 v+1}{v^{2}+v+1} d v+\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{d v}{v^{2}+v+1}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \log \left|v^{2}+v+1\right|+\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{d v}{v^{2}+v+\frac{1}{4}+1-\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \log \left|v^{2}+v+1\right|{/tex}+ {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{d v}{\left(v+\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \log \left|v^{2}+v+1\right|{/tex}+ {tex}\frac{3}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)} \tan ^{-1} \frac{\left(v+\frac{1}{2}\right)}{\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \log \left|v^{2}+v+1\right|{/tex}+ {tex}\sqrt{3} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 v+1}{\sqrt{3}}\right){/tex}
From (1), we get
{tex}-\frac{1}{2} \log \left|v^{2}+v+1\right|{/tex}+ {tex}\sqrt{3} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 v+1}{\sqrt{3}}\right){/tex} = logx + C
Putting v = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex}, we get
{tex}\sqrt{3} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\frac{2 y}{x}+1}{\sqrt{3}}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \log \left(\frac{y^{2}}{x^{2}}+\frac{y}{x}+1\right){/tex}= logx + C
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{3} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 y+x}{\sqrt{3} x}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \log \left(\frac{y^{2}+x y+x^{2}}{x^{2}}\right){/tex} = logx + C....(2)
Put x = 1 and y = 0 in the above equation, we get
{tex}\sqrt{3} \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{2 \times 0+1}{\sqrt{3} \times 1}\right]{/tex} - {tex}\frac{1}{2} \log \left[\frac{0+0+1}{1}\right]{/tex} = log 1 + C
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{3} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \log 1{/tex} = log 1 + C
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{3} \times \frac{\pi}{6}-0{/tex} = 0 + C .....[as log 1 = 0]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3} \pi}{6}{/tex}
Now, putting the value of C in (2) equation, we get
 {tex}\sqrt{3} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 y+x}{\sqrt{3} x}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \log \left(\frac{y^{2}+x y+x^{2}}{x^{2}}\right){/tex} = logx + {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3} \pi}{6}{/tex}

question_text: Solve the differential equation: {tex}\left( {1 + {e^{\frac{x}{y}}}} \right)dx + {e^{\frac{x}{y}}}\left( {1 - \frac{x}{y}} \right)dy = 0{/tex}
explanation: {tex}\left( {1 + {e^{\frac{x}{y}}}} \right)dx + {e^{\frac{x}{y}}}\left( {1 - \frac{x}{y}} \right)dy = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = - \frac{{{e^{x/y}}\left( {1 - \frac{x}{y}} \right)}}{{1 + {e^{x/y}}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = \frac{{{e^{x/y}}\left( {\frac{x}{y} - 1} \right)}}{{1 + {e^{x/y}}}}{/tex} ........(1) Let x = vy, then, {tex}\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = v + y\frac{{dv}}{{dy}}{/tex} Put {tex}\frac{{dx}}{{dy}}{/tex} in eq (1),we get, {tex}v + y \frac{{dv}}{{dy}} = \frac{{{e^v}(v - 1)}}{{{e^v} + 1}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y\frac{{dv}}{{dy}} = \frac{{v{e^v} - {e^v}}}{{{e^v} + 1}} - v{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y\frac{{dv}}{{dy}} = \frac{{v{e^v} - {e^v} - v{e^v} - v}}{{{e^v} + 1}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow- \int {\frac{{dy}}{y}} = \int {\frac{{{e^v} + 1}}{{v + {e^v}}}dv} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\log ({e^v} + v) = - \log (y) + c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\log (({e^v} + v).y) = c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow({e^v} + v)y = {e^c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow({e^v} + v)y = A{/tex} [ Putting e c = A ] {tex}\Rightarrow\left( {{e^{x/y}} + \frac{x}{y}} \right)y = A{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y{e^{x/y}} + x = A{/tex}
solution: {tex}\left( {1 + {e^{\frac{x}{y}}}} \right)dx + {e^{\frac{x}{y}}}\left( {1 - \frac{x}{y}} \right)dy = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = - \frac{{{e^{x/y}}\left( {1 - \frac{x}{y}} \right)}}{{1 + {e^{x/y}}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = \frac{{{e^{x/y}}\left( {\frac{x}{y} - 1} \right)}}{{1 + {e^{x/y}}}}{/tex} ........(1) Let x = vy, then, {tex}\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = v + y\frac{{dv}}{{dy}}{/tex} Put {tex}\frac{{dx}}{{dy}}{/tex} in eq (1),we get, {tex}v + y \frac{{dv}}{{dy}} = \frac{{{e^v}(v - 1)}}{{{e^v} + 1}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y\frac{{dv}}{{dy}} = \frac{{v{e^v} - {e^v}}}{{{e^v} + 1}} - v{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y\frac{{dv}}{{dy}} = \frac{{v{e^v} - {e^v} - v{e^v} - v}}{{{e^v} + 1}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow- \int {\frac{{dy}}{y}} = \int {\frac{{{e^v} + 1}}{{v + {e^v}}}dv} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\log ({e^v} + v) = - \log (y) + c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\log (({e^v} + v).y) = c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow({e^v} + v)y = {e^c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow({e^v} + v)y = A{/tex} [ Putting e c = A ] {tex}\Rightarrow\left( {{e^{x/y}} + \frac{x}{y}} \right)y = A{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y{e^{x/y}} + x = A{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = - \frac{{{e^{x/y}}\left( {1 - \frac{x}{y}} \right)}}{{1 + {e^{x/y}}}}{/tex}

{tex}\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = v + y\frac{{dv}}{{dy}}{/tex}

= k0f(x,y)

log t
log(ev + v)

yex/y + x = C
x + yex/y = C

question_text: Show that the differential equation of {tex}\left(x^{2}+x y\right) d y=\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right) d x{/tex} is homogeneous and solve it.
explanation: We have {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}+x y}{/tex} Let {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}+x y}{/tex} Here, putting x = kx and y = ky {tex}f(k x, k y)=\frac{(k x)^{2}+(k y)^{2}}{(k x)^{2}+k x . k y}{/tex} = k 0 .f(x,y) Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. (x 2 + xy)dy = (x 2 + y 2 )dx {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}+x y}{/tex} To solve it we make the substitution. y = vx Differentiating above eq. with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+(v x)^{2}}{x^{2}+x \cdot v x}{/tex} {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}\left(1+v^{2}\right)}{x^{2}(1+v)}{/tex} {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{1+v^{2}}{1+v}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{1+\mathrm{v}^{2}}{1+\mathrm{v}}-\mathrm{v}=\frac{1+\mathrm{v}^{2}-\mathrm{v}-\mathrm{v}^{2}}{1+\mathrm{v}}{/tex} {tex}x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{1-v}{1+v}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1+v}{1-v} d v=\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} Integrating on both side, {tex}\int \frac{1+v}{1-v} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\int\left(-1+\frac{2}{1-v}\right) d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} - v - 2log|1 - v| = log|x| + log c {tex}-\frac{y}{x}-2 \log \left|1-\frac{y}{x}\right|=\log |x|+\log C{/tex} {tex}-\frac{y}{x}=2 \log \left|1-\frac{y}{x}\right|+\log |x|+\log C{/tex} {tex}-\frac{y}{x}=\log \frac{(x-y)^{2}}{x^{2}}+\log |x|+\log C{/tex} {tex}-\frac{y}{x}=\log \frac{(x-y)^{2}}{x^{2}} \cdot C x{/tex} {tex}-\frac{y}{x}=\log \frac{(x-y)^{2}}{x} c{/tex} {tex}-\frac{y}{x}=\log \frac{(x-y)^{2}}{x} c{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y})^{2}}{\mathrm{x}}=\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{y} / \mathrm{x}}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y})^{2}=\mathrm{xe}^{-\mathrm{y} / \mathrm{x}}{/tex} {tex}(x-y)^{2}=k x e^{-y / x}{/tex} Which is the required solution of the given differential equation.

We have {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}+x y}{/tex} 
Let {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}+x y}{/tex} 
Here, putting x = kx and y = ky
{tex}f(k x, k y)=\frac{(k x)^{2}+(k y)^{2}}{(k x)^{2}+k x . k y}{/tex} = k0.f(x,y)
Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous.
(x2 + xy)dy = (x2 + y2)dx 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}+x y}{/tex} 
To solve it we make the substitution.
y = vx
Differentiating above eq. with respect to x, we get
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} 
{tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+(v x)^{2}}{x^{2}+x \cdot v x}{/tex} 
{tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}\left(1+v^{2}\right)}{x^{2}(1+v)}{/tex} 
{tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{1+v^{2}}{1+v}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{1+\mathrm{v}^{2}}{1+\mathrm{v}}-\mathrm{v}=\frac{1+\mathrm{v}^{2}-\mathrm{v}-\mathrm{v}^{2}}{1+\mathrm{v}}{/tex} 
{tex}x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{1-v}{1+v}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1+v}{1-v} d v=\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
Integrating on both side,
{tex}\int \frac{1+v}{1-v} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\int\left(-1+\frac{2}{1-v}\right) d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
- v - 2log|1 - v| = log|x| + log c 
{tex}-\frac{y}{x}-2 \log \left|1-\frac{y}{x}\right|=\log |x|+\log C{/tex} 
{tex}-\frac{y}{x}=2 \log \left|1-\frac{y}{x}\right|+\log |x|+\log C{/tex} 
{tex}-\frac{y}{x}=\log \frac{(x-y)^{2}}{x^{2}}+\log |x|+\log C{/tex} 
{tex}-\frac{y}{x}=\log \frac{(x-y)^{2}}{x^{2}} \cdot C x{/tex} 
{tex}-\frac{y}{x}=\log \frac{(x-y)^{2}}{x} c{/tex} 
{tex}-\frac{y}{x}=\log \frac{(x-y)^{2}}{x} c{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y})^{2}}{\mathrm{x}}=\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{y} / \mathrm{x}}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y})^{2}=\mathrm{xe}^{-\mathrm{y} / \mathrm{x}}{/tex} 
{tex}(x-y)^{2}=k x e^{-y / x}{/tex} 
Which is the required solution of the given differential equation.

question_text: Solve the following differential equation. {tex}\sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } + y ^ { 2 } + x ^ { 2 } y ^ { 2 } } + x y \frac { d y } { d x } = 0{/tex}
explanation: Given differential equation is: {tex}\sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } + y ^ { 2 } + x ^ { 2 } y ^ { 2 } } + x y \frac { d y } { d x } = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { 1 \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) + y ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) } = - x y \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) \left( 1 + y ^ { 2 } \right) } = - x y \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } = - x y \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { y } { \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } } d y = - \frac { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } } { x } d x{/tex} Therefore,on integrating both sides, we get, {tex}\int \frac { y } { \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } } d y = - \int \frac { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } } { x ^ { 2 } } x d x{/tex} Therefore,on putting 1 + y 2 = t and 1 + x 2 = u 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2y dy = dt and 2x dx = 2u du {tex}\Rightarrow y d y = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex} and x dx = u du {tex}\therefore ~~ \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { d t } { \sqrt { t } } = - \int \frac { u } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } \cdot u d u{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int t ^ { - 1 / 2 } d t = - \int \frac { u ^ { 2 } } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 2 } \frac { t ^ { 1 / 2 } } { 1 / 2 } = - \int \frac { \left( u ^ { 2 } - 1 + 1 \right) } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow t ^ { 1 / 2 } = - \int \frac { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u - \int \frac { 1 } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u{/tex} ...[put 1 + y 2 = t] {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } = - u - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| \frac { u - 1 } { u + 1 } \right| + C{/tex} ... {tex}\left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } - a ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { 2 a } \log \left| \frac { x - a } { x + a } \right| \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } = - \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| \frac { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } - 1 } { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } + 1 } \right| + C{/tex}
solution: Given differential equation is: {tex}\sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } + y ^ { 2 } + x ^ { 2 } y ^ { 2 } } + x y \frac { d y } { d x } = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { 1 \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) + y ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) } = - x y \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) \left( 1 + y ^ { 2 } \right) } = - x y \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } = - x y \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { y } { \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } } d y = - \frac { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } } { x } d x{/tex} Therefore,on integrating both sides, we get, {tex}\int \frac { y } { \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } } d y = - \int \frac { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } } { x ^ { 2 } } x d x{/tex} Therefore,on putting 1 + y 2 = t and 1 + x 2 = u 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2y dy = dt and 2x dx = 2u du {tex}\Rightarrow y d y = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex} and x dx = u du {tex}\therefore ~~ \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { d t } { \sqrt { t } } = - \int \frac { u } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } \cdot u d u{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int t ^ { - 1 / 2 } d t = - \int \frac { u ^ { 2 } } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 2 } \frac { t ^ { 1 / 2 } } { 1 / 2 } = - \int \frac { \left( u ^ { 2 } - 1 + 1 \right) } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow t ^ { 1 / 2 } = - \int \frac { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u - \int \frac { 1 } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u{/tex} ...[put 1 + y 2 = t] {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } = - u - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| \frac { u - 1 } { u + 1 } \right| + C{/tex} ... {tex}\left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } - a ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { 2 a } \log \left| \frac { x - a } { x + a } \right| \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } = - \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| \frac { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } - 1 } { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } + 1 } \right| + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given differential equation is:
{tex}\sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } + y ^ { 2 } + x ^ { 2 } y ^ { 2 } } + x y \frac { d y } { d x } = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { 1 \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) + y ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) } = - x y \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) \left( 1 + y ^ { 2 } \right) } = - x y \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } = - x y \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { y } { \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } } d y = - \frac { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } } { x } d x{/tex}
Therefore,on integrating both sides, we get,
{tex}\int \frac { y } { \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } } d y = - \int \frac { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } } { x ^ { 2 } } x d x{/tex}
Therefore,on putting 1 + y2 = t and 1 + x2 = u2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2y dy = dt and 2x dx = 2u du
{tex}\Rightarrow y d y = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex} and x dx = u du
{tex}\therefore ~~ \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { d t } { \sqrt { t } } = - \int \frac { u } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } \cdot u d u{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int t ^ { - 1 / 2 } d t = - \int \frac { u ^ { 2 } } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 2 } \frac { t ^ { 1 / 2 } } { 1 / 2 } = - \int \frac { \left( u ^ { 2 } - 1 + 1 \right) } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow t ^ { 1 / 2 } = - \int \frac { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u - \int \frac { 1 } { u ^ { 2 } - 1 } d u{/tex}    ...[put 1 + y2 = t]
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } = - u - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| \frac { u - 1 } { u + 1 } \right| + C{/tex}    ...{tex}\left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } - a ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { 2 a } \log \left| \frac { x - a } { x + a } \right| \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \sqrt { 1 + y ^ { 2 } } = - \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| \frac { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } - 1 } { \sqrt { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } + 1 } \right| + C{/tex}

question_html: Show that the differential equation of {tex}\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) d x+2 x y d y=0{/tex} is homogeneous and solve it.
question_text: Show that the differential equation of {tex}\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) d x+2 x y d y=0{/tex} is homogeneous and solve it.
explanation: We have {tex}2 x y d y=-\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} Let {tex}f(x, y)=-\frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} Here, putting x = kx and y = ky {tex}f(k x, k y)=-\frac{k^{2} x^{2}-k^{2} y^{2}}{2 k^{2} x y}{/tex} {tex}f(k x, k y)=-\frac{k^{2}}{k^{2}} \cdot \frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} = k 0 .f(x,y) Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. {tex}\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) d x+2 x y d y=0{/tex} {tex}2 x y d y=-\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} To solve it we make the substitution. y = vx Differentiating eq. with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}-\mathrm{v}^{2} \mathrm{x}^{2}}{2 \mathrm{x} \cdot \mathrm{vx}}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}\left(1-\mathrm{v}^{2}\right)}{2 \mathrm{vx}^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{v}^{2}-1}{2 \mathrm{v}}-\mathrm{v}{/tex} {tex}x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{-1-v^{2}}{2 v}{/tex} {tex}-\frac{2 \mathrm{v}}{1+\mathrm{v}^{2}} \mathrm{dv}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} Integrating both sides, we get {tex}\int \frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} .......(i) Let {tex}I_{1}=\int \frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v{/tex} Put 1 + v 2 = t 2vdv = dt {tex}\mathrm{vdv}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{dt}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v=\int \frac{1}{t} d t{/tex} = log(t) {tex}\therefore{/tex} log(1 + v 2 ) = -logx + logC ( {tex}\therefore{/tex} From (i) eq.) {tex}\log \left(1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right)=-\log x+\log c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x^{2}+y^{2}=C x{/tex} is the required solution of the differential equation.
solution: We have {tex}2 x y d y=-\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} Let {tex}f(x, y)=-\frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} Here, putting x = kx and y = ky {tex}f(k x, k y)=-\frac{k^{2} x^{2}-k^{2} y^{2}}{2 k^{2} x y}{/tex} {tex}f(k x, k y)=-\frac{k^{2}}{k^{2}} \cdot \frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} = k 0 .f(x,y) Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. {tex}\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) d x+2 x y d y=0{/tex} {tex}2 x y d y=-\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} To solve it we make the substitution. y = vx Differentiating eq. with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}-\mathrm{v}^{2} \mathrm{x}^{2}}{2 \mathrm{x} \cdot \mathrm{vx}}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}\left(1-\mathrm{v}^{2}\right)}{2 \mathrm{vx}^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{v}^{2}-1}{2 \mathrm{v}}-\mathrm{v}{/tex} {tex}x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{-1-v^{2}}{2 v}{/tex} {tex}-\frac{2 \mathrm{v}}{1+\mathrm{v}^{2}} \mathrm{dv}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} Integrating both sides, we get {tex}\int \frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} .......(i) Let {tex}I_{1}=\int \frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v{/tex} Put 1 + v 2 = t 2vdv = dt {tex}\mathrm{vdv}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{dt}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v=\int \frac{1}{t} d t{/tex} = log(t) {tex}\therefore{/tex} log(1 + v 2 ) = -logx + logC ( {tex}\therefore{/tex} From (i) eq.) {tex}\log \left(1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right)=-\log x+\log c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x^{2}+y^{2}=C x{/tex} is the required solution of the differential equation.
solution_html:
We have 
{tex}2 x y d y=-\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) d x{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} 
Let {tex}f(x, y)=-\frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} 
Here, putting x = kx and y = ky
{tex}f(k x, k y)=-\frac{k^{2} x^{2}-k^{2} y^{2}}{2 k^{2} x y}{/tex} 
{tex}f(k x, k y)=-\frac{k^{2}}{k^{2}} \cdot \frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} 
= k0.f(x,y)
Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous.
{tex}\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) d x+2 x y d y=0{/tex} 
{tex}2 x y d y=-\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) d x{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{x^{2}-y^{2}}{2 x y}{/tex} 
To solve it we make the substitution.
y = vx
Differentiating eq. with respect to x, we get
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}-\mathrm{v}^{2} \mathrm{x}^{2}}{2 \mathrm{x} \cdot \mathrm{vx}}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}\left(1-\mathrm{v}^{2}\right)}{2 \mathrm{vx}^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{v}^{2}-1}{2 \mathrm{v}}-\mathrm{v}{/tex} 
{tex}x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{-1-v^{2}}{2 v}{/tex} 
{tex}-\frac{2 \mathrm{v}}{1+\mathrm{v}^{2}} \mathrm{dv}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
Integrating both sides, we get
{tex}\int \frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} .......(i)
Let {tex}I_{1}=\int \frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v{/tex} 
Put 1 + v2 = t
2vdv = dt
{tex}\mathrm{vdv}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{dt}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{2 v}{1+v^{2}} d v=\int \frac{1}{t} d t{/tex} = log(t)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} log(1 + v2) = -logx + logC ({tex}\therefore{/tex} From (i) eq.)
{tex}\log \left(1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right)=-\log x+\log c{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow x^{2}+y^{2}=C x{/tex}  is the required solution of the differential equation.

question_html: Solve the differential equation: {tex}x^{2} y d x-\left(x^{3}+y^{3}\right) d y=0{/tex}
question_text: Solve the differential equation: {tex}x^{2} y d x-\left(x^{3}+y^{3}\right) d y=0{/tex}
explanation: Given differential equation can be written as {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y x^{2}}{x^{3}+y^{3}}{/tex} Put {tex}y=v x{/tex} , so {tex}\frac{d v}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} Therefore, {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{v x^{3}}{x^{3}+v^{3} x^{3}}=\frac{v}{1+v^{3}}{/tex} {tex}x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{-v^{4}}{1+v^{3}}{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{1}{v^{4}}+\frac{1}{v}\right) d v=\frac{-d x}{x}{/tex} Integrating we get {tex}\frac{-1}{3 v^{3}}+\log |v|=-\log |x|+C{/tex} {tex}\frac{-{x}^{3}}{3 {y}^{3}}+\log |y|={C}{/tex}
solution: Given differential equation can be written as {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y x^{2}}{x^{3}+y^{3}}{/tex} Put {tex}y=v x{/tex} , so {tex}\frac{d v}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} Therefore, {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{v x^{3}}{x^{3}+v^{3} x^{3}}=\frac{v}{1+v^{3}}{/tex} {tex}x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{-v^{4}}{1+v^{3}}{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{1}{v^{4}}+\frac{1}{v}\right) d v=\frac{-d x}{x}{/tex} Integrating we get {tex}\frac{-1}{3 v^{3}}+\log |v|=-\log |x|+C{/tex} {tex}\frac{-{x}^{3}}{3 {y}^{3}}+\log |y|={C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given differential equation can be written as {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y x^{2}}{x^{3}+y^{3}}{/tex}
Put {tex}y=v x{/tex}, so {tex}\frac{d v}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{v x^{3}}{x^{3}+v^{3} x^{3}}=\frac{v}{1+v^{3}}{/tex}
{tex}x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{-v^{4}}{1+v^{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\left(\frac{1}{v^{4}}+\frac{1}{v}\right) d v=\frac{-d x}{x}{/tex}
Integrating we get
{tex}\frac{-1}{3 v^{3}}+\log |v|=-\log |x|+C{/tex}
{tex}\frac{-{x}^{3}}{3 {y}^{3}}+\log |y|={C}{/tex}

question_text: Show that the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}-y+x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0{/tex} is homogeneous and solve it.
explanation: We have {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}=y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{x}{/tex} Let {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{x}{/tex} Here, putting x = kx and y = ky {tex}f(\mathrm{kx}, \mathrm{ky})=\frac{\mathrm{ky}-\mathrm{kx} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{ky}}{\mathrm{kx}}\right)}{\mathrm{kx}}{/tex} {tex}f(\mathrm{kx}, \mathrm{ky})=\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{k}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{y}-\mathrm{x} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex} = k 0 .f(x,y) Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{y}-\mathrm{xsin}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{x}{/tex} To solve it we make the substitution. y = vx Differentiating eq. with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{vx}-\mathrm{xsin}\left(\frac{\mathrm{vx}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)}{\mathrm{x}} {/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{v}-\sin \mathrm{v}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\sin \mathrm{v}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\sin v} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}cosec \ v dv =-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} Integrating both side, we get {tex}\int cosec v d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} log(cosecv – cotv) = -logx + logC {tex}\log \left(cosec \frac{y}{x}-\cot \frac{y}{x}\right)=\log \frac{c}{x}{/tex} {tex}cosec \frac{y}{x}-\cot \frac{y}{x}=\frac{C}{x}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\sin \frac{y}{x}}-\frac{\cos \frac{y}{x}}{\sin \frac{y}{x}}=\frac{c}{x}{/tex} {tex}1-\cos \frac{y}{x}=\frac{C}{x} \cdot \sin \frac{y}{x}{/tex} {tex}x\left(1-\cos \frac{y}{x}\right)=\operatorname{csin} \frac{y}{x}{/tex} The required solution of the differential equation.
solution: We have {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}=y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{x}{/tex} Let {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{x}{/tex} Here, putting x = kx and y = ky {tex}f(\mathrm{kx}, \mathrm{ky})=\frac{\mathrm{ky}-\mathrm{kx} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{ky}}{\mathrm{kx}}\right)}{\mathrm{kx}}{/tex} {tex}f(\mathrm{kx}, \mathrm{ky})=\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{k}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{y}-\mathrm{x} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex} = k 0 .f(x,y) Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{y}-\mathrm{xsin}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{x}{/tex} To solve it we make the substitution. y = vx Differentiating eq. with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{vx}-\mathrm{xsin}\left(\frac{\mathrm{vx}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)}{\mathrm{x}} {/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{v}-\sin \mathrm{v}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\sin \mathrm{v}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\sin v} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}cosec \ v dv =-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} Integrating both side, we get {tex}\int cosec v d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} log(cosecv – cotv) = -logx + logC {tex}\log \left(cosec \frac{y}{x}-\cot \frac{y}{x}\right)=\log \frac{c}{x}{/tex} {tex}cosec \frac{y}{x}-\cot \frac{y}{x}=\frac{C}{x}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\sin \frac{y}{x}}-\frac{\cos \frac{y}{x}}{\sin \frac{y}{x}}=\frac{c}{x}{/tex} {tex}1-\cos \frac{y}{x}=\frac{C}{x} \cdot \sin \frac{y}{x}{/tex} {tex}x\left(1-\cos \frac{y}{x}\right)=\operatorname{csin} \frac{y}{x}{/tex} The required solution of the differential equation.
solution_html:
We have
{tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}=y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{x}{/tex} 
Let {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{x}{/tex} 
Here, putting x = kx and y = ky
{tex}f(\mathrm{kx}, \mathrm{ky})=\frac{\mathrm{ky}-\mathrm{kx} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{ky}}{\mathrm{kx}}\right)}{\mathrm{kx}}{/tex} 
{tex}f(\mathrm{kx}, \mathrm{ky})=\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{k}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{y}-\mathrm{x} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex} 
= k0.f(x,y)
Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous.
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{y}-\mathrm{xsin}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y-x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{x}{/tex}

(x + y)dy + (x - y)dx = 0
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{(x-y)}{(x+y)}{/tex} 
Let {tex}f(x, y)=-\frac{(x-y)}{(x+y)}{/tex} 
Here, putting x= kx and y = ky
{tex}f(\mathrm{kx}, \mathrm{ky})=-\frac{(\mathrm{kx}-\mathrm{ky})}{(\mathrm{kx}+\mathrm{ky})}{/tex} = k0.f(x,y)
Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous.
Now,
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{(x-y)}{(x+y)}{/tex} 
To solve it we make the substitution.
y = vx ...(i)
Differentiating equation (i), with respect to x, we get
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{vx})}{(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{vx})}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{(1-\mathrm{v})}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{(1-\mathrm{v})}{(1+\mathrm{v})}-\mathrm{v}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-1+\mathrm{v}-\mathrm{v}-\mathrm{v}^{2}}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-1-\mathrm{v}^{2}}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-\left(1+\mathrm{v}^{2}\right)}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1+v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
Integrating both sides, we get
{tex}\int \frac{1+v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\int \frac{1}{1+v^{2}} d v+\int \frac{v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\tan ^{-1} v+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+v^{2}\right)=-\log x+C{/tex} 
{tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right)=-\log x+C{/tex} 
y = 1 when x = 1

{tex}\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2=0+c{/tex} 
{tex}C=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore \tan ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)^{2}\right)=-\log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} 
where, {tex}C=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}-\log x+\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex} 
{tex}2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}-2 \log x+\frac{\pi}{2}+\log 2{/tex}
{tex}2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}}\right)+\log x^{2}=\frac{\pi}{2}+\log 2{/tex} 
{tex}2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}+\log 2{/tex}
